title: Swipe & Study
summary: A tutor matching platform that connects students with tutors through an intuitive, swipe-based interface — blending gamification, real-time interaction, and user-centric design to transform academic support.

challenges.title: Swipe-Based Tutor Matching
challenges.problem: Building a Tinder-like card swiping experience that feels fluid while maintaining tutor profile density and preventing accidental swipes
challenges.solution: Custom UIView with pan gesture recognizer and snap-back animation. Cards stack with transform scale/rotation — left swipes dismiss, right swipes trigger match animation and push notification to tutor

challenges.title: Moolah Wallet & In-App Currency
challenges.problem: Students need to purchase in-app currency for premium features — transactions must be secure, trackable, and auditable without exposing payment logic on the client
challenges.solution: Firebase Functions handle purchase verification via StoreKit receipt validation. Wallet balance stored server-side in Firestore with transaction history; client reads only via server-backed queries

challenges.title: Real-Time Messaging Between Matched Pairs
challenges.problem: Students and tutors need instant messaging after matching — messages must persist, show delivery status, and enforce read receipts without excessive Firestore reads
challenges.solution: Firebase Firestore listener on a per-match subcollection. Messages batched with server timestamps; unread count aggregated via Cloud Function on write. Typing indicators use a debounced presence field

challenges.title: Appointment & Session Scheduling
challenges.problem: Students and tutors operate on different timezones and availability windows — double-booking and missed sessions must be prevented
challenges.solution: CalendarKit-based availability picker stores weekly recurring slots per tutor. Booking request creates a pending session; tutor must confirm within 24h or it auto-cancels. iCal export for device calendar sync

challenges.title: Post-Session Feedback & Ratings
challenges.problem: Both parties need accountability but anonymous ratings can be abused — must detect and prevent retaliatory low ratings
challenges.solution: Ratings revealed only after both parties submit. If one party doesn't rate within 72h, average defaults. Pattern detection in Cloud Functions flags suspicious rating sequences for manual review

challenges.title: Premium Upgrades & Subscription Tiers
challenges.problem: Different feature sets for free vs premium users — paywall logic must be server-driven to prevent client-side tampering
challenges.solution: StoreKit 2 Entitlement checked at app launch; premium features gated via remote config flags from Firestore. Restore purchases flow with AppTransaction verification. Feature flags toggle UI elements without recompilation
